COVER_BODY: A single-night, photo-led music film of modular vertical shorts. Portland, scored to SUPERCUT (3:12).
CONCEPT_LEDE: The film splits at the drop. One rehearsed street, walked until it goes wrong; then the night's own cameras take over and tell the rest.
SHORTS_LEDE: Each clip stands alone as a vertical and composes into the 3:12 hero. Different source camera, movement, and grammar per cut.
TONE_A_BODY: The loop block is composed like still photography: locked frames, patient blocking, wrongness you find on the second look.
TONE_B_BODY: Found-footage grammar with real-world sources only. If a camera could not exist in the scene, its shot does not exist in the film.
GRADE_LEDE: Five colors carry the film across paper, screen, and grade. The volt accent marks every loop reset.
SPLIT_BODY: A hundred meters of Frame Street, locked white balance, identical framing every pass. Kilo walks; the city resets around him. The audience learns the frame so well that a moved trash can reads like a jump scare.
SPLIT_CHAR: Available sodium light + one hidden bounce · locked tripod · matched blocking marks · 5600K held across passes · wrongness from staging, never from grade
